steps.title: Apply the Pythagorean Theorem
steps.content:
For a right triangle with legs and hypotenuse, we apply the theorem. We know one leg is $43.2$ mm and the hypotenuse is $196.8$ mm. Setting up the equation with unknown leg $b$:
$$43.2^2 + b^2 = 196.8^2$$
Rearranging to solve for $b^2$:
$$b^2 = 196.8^2 - 43.2^2$$

steps.title: Calculate the Other Leg
steps.content:
Evaluate the squared values:
$$196.8^2 = 38730.24$$
$$43.2^2 = 1866.24$$
Substitute into the rearranged equation:
$$b^2 = 38730.24 - 1866.24 = 36864$$
Take the square root:
$$b = \sqrt{36864} = 192$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
